success_surface: Efficient bulk row filtering using pandas vectorized operations instead of iterative cell manipulation
effective_behaviors: Selected pandas library for high-volume row filtering operations requiring conditional logic
Applied vectorized filtering methods (isin, boolean indexing) rather than loop-based iteration
Preserved source file integrity by writing results to a new output file path
Verified transformation success through before/after state logging (row counts, sample data)
Identified target worksheet name from task instructions before accessing the spreadsheet
evidence: Used pandas.read_excel() to load entire worksheet into DataFrame for batch processing
Applied df[~df['TYPE'].isin(['STATIONERY', 'VEGETABLES'])] for efficient conditional filtering
Printed original and filtered DataFrame shapes to confirm correct number of rows deleted
Saved output to separate path rather than modifying input file in-place
Correctly specified sheet_name='Delete' matching the task's target worksheet

memory_items.title: Performance Optimization Through Vectorization
memory_items.lesson: For bulk filtering or conditional row operations, use pandas vectorized methods instead of iterative cell-by-cell loops
memory_items.generalization: When deleting, filtering, or transforming many rows based on column values, leverage boolean indexing and .isin() methods which execute significantly faster than For/While loops over individual cells

memory_items.title: Source File Integrity Preservation
memory_items.lesson: Always write processed results to a new file path rather than modifying the original spreadsheet
memory_items.generalization: Create output paths that differ from input paths, and verify the output directory exists before writing to prevent data loss

memory_items.title: Transformation Verification Practices
memory_items.lesson: Log before/after state information to confirm the expected number of changes occurred
memory_items.generalization: Report original dimensions, modified dimensions, and count of affected rows/columns after data manipulation to validate correctness

memory_items.title: Library Selection for Processing Scale
memory_items.lesson: Choose pandas for data-heavy operations and openpyxl only when formatting/chart preservation is required
memory_items.generalization: For pure data transformation tasks without styling needs, pandas alone provides better performance and simpler code than mixing libraries

edits.content:
## 6. Performance Optimization
- **Vectorization Priority**: For bulk row operations (filtering, deletion, updates), prefer pandas vectorized methods over loop-based cell iteration. Use boolean indexing and `.isin()` for conditional filtering.
- **Scale-Aware Tool Selection**: Choose pandas exclusively for data-only transformations; reserve openpyxl for cases requiring format/style/chart preservation. Mixed usage adds overhead unless formatting must be retained.
- **Batch Processing Confirmation**: Log original and final row/column counts, plus count of affected entries, to verify transformation magnitude matches expectations.
- **Output Separation**: Always write transformed data to a distinct output file rather than modifying the source workbook directly.
